title: festuca pumila var. varia
intro: Festuca pumila var. varia, also known as dwarf fescue or alpine fescue, is a low-growing, perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds. Festuca pumila var. varia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and to aid in the treatment of various infections.
